questions.q: If the line $\frac{x-1}{2}=\frac{y+2}{3}=\frac{z-4}{6}$ intersect the $x y, y z$ and $z x$ planes at $A, B$ and $C$ respectively, and if volume of the tetrahedron OABD is V , where ' O ' is origin and D is the image of C in the x -axis, then the value of $[\mathrm{V}]$ is $\qquad$ . (Where [.] denote greatest integer function).
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A plane $P$ passes through the intersection of $\dot{r} \cdot(\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k})+3=0$ and $\dot{r} \cdot(\hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k})=2$. If $p$ divide the line segment joining $(3,0,2)$ and $(0,3,-1)$ in the ratio $2: 1$ internally and the equation of plane is given by $\vec{r} \cdot(a \hat{i}-\hat{j}+b \hat{k})=c$ when $a, b, c \in N$ than $a+b+c$ is/are $\qquad$
questions.solution:
Required plane is $p_1+\lambda p_2=0$ and passes through $(1,2,0)$
$\Rightarrow p=\vec{r} \cdot(3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+3 \hat{k})=1) \Rightarrow a+b+c=7$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two parallel planes are given by, $x+y+z=1$ and $x+y+z=\frac{9}{2}$. A third plane that intersects them is given by $2 x-5 y+z=-5$, resulting in two parallel lines of intersection. If the distance ' $d$ ' between these two parallel lines can be expressed as $d=\sqrt{\frac{a}{b}}$, where $a$ and $b$ are co-prime positive integers, then find the value of $[d]$. [Note: Where $[k]$ denotes greatest integer function less than or equal to $k$.]
questions.solution:
$$ P_1: x+y+z=1 ; \quad P_2: x+y+z=\frac{9}{2} \quad \text { and } \quad P_3: 2 x-5 y+z=-5 $$ Vector along the line of intersection of the planes $P_1 / P_3$ or $P_2 / P_3$ is $$ =\overrightarrow{\mathbf{n}}=\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \hat{\mathbf{i}} & \hat{\mathbf{j}} & \hat{\mathbf{k}} \\ 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 2 & -5 & 1 \end{array}\right|=6 \hat{\mathbf{i}}+\hat{\mathbf{j}}-7 \hat{\mathbf{k}} $$ Point on line $L_1$ which is the line of intersection of $P_1$ and $P_3$ say $\left(x_1, y_1, 0\right)$ Hence, $x_1+y_1=1$ and $2 x_1-5 y_1=-5$ Solving, we get $y_1=1$ and $x_1=0$ Hence, point on $L_1$ is $(0,1,0)$......(A) ||ly point on line $L_2$ which is line of intersection of $P_2$ and $P_3$ say $\left(x_2, y_2, 0\right)$ Hence $x_2+y_2=\frac{9}{2}$ and $2 x_2-5 y_2=-5$ Solving, we get $y_2=2$ and $x_2=\frac{5}{2}$ Hence $x_2+y_2=\frac{9}{2}$ and $2 x_2-5 y_2=-5$ Solving, we get $y_2=2$ and $x_2=\frac{5}{2}$ Hence, point on $L_2$ is $(5 / 2,2,0)$ Now $d=|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{v}}| \sin \theta=\frac{|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{v}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}}|}{|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}}|}=\frac{\sqrt{|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{v}}|^2|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}}|^2-(\overrightarrow{\mathbf{v}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}})^2}}{|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}}|}$ ...(1) Now $|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{v}}|=\sqrt{\frac{25}{4}+1}=\sqrt{\frac{29}{4}} ; \quad|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}}|=\sqrt{86}$ $$ \overrightarrow{\mathbf{v}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}}=16 $$
Now from equation (1), $\quad d=\sqrt{\frac{\frac{29}{4} \times 86-256}{86}}=\sqrt{\frac{\frac{29}{4} \times 86-256}{86}}$ $$ =\sqrt{\frac{735}{2 \times 86}}=\sqrt{4.27}=2.06 $$ $\begin{array}{ll}\therefore & {[d]=2}\end{array}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The plane denoted by $\pi_1: 4 x+7 y+4 z+81=0$ is rotated through a right angle about its line of intersection with the plane $\pi_2: 5 x+3 y+10 z=25$. If the plane in its new position be denoted as $\pi$, and the distance of this plane from origin is $\sqrt{\mathrm{k}}$, where $\mathrm{k} \in \mathrm{N}$, then $(\mathrm{k}-12) / 100$ is $\qquad$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& 4 x+7 y+4 z+81=0....(1) \\
& 5 x+3 y+10 z=25.....(2)
\end{aligned}$

Equation of plane passing through intersection of (1) and (2)
$\begin{aligned}
& (4 x+7 y++z+81)+\lambda(5 x+3 y+10 z-25)=0 \\
& \Rightarrow(4+5 \lambda) k+(7+3 \lambda) y+(4+10 \lambda) z+81-25 \lambda=0
\end{aligned}$

Plane (3) perpendicular to (1)
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { So. } 4(4+5 \lambda)+7(7+3 \lambda)+4(4+10 \lambda)=0 \\
& \Rightarrow \lambda=-1 \\
& \text { Putting } \lambda=-1 \text { in }(3) \\
& -x+4 y-6 z+106=0....(4)
\end{aligned}$
$\therefore$ distance of (4) from $(0,0,0)$
$\begin{aligned}
& \sqrt{k}=\frac{106}{\sqrt{1+16+36}} \\
& \Rightarrow k=212
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: $P$ is a point on the plane $a x+b y+c z=d$. A point $Q$ is taken on the line $O P$ such $O P . O Q=d^2$, then the locus of $Q$ satisfies $\frac{d(a x+b y+c z)}{x^2+y^2+z^2}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Let $P\left(x_1, y_1, z_1\right)$ be a point on $a x+b y+c z=d$...(1) 
Then $\mathrm{ax}_1+\mathrm{by}_1+\mathrm{cz}_1=\mathrm{d}$
Let $O P=r$. Then direction cosines of $O P$ are $\frac{x_1}{r} \cdot \frac{y_1}{r}, \frac{z_1}{r}$ Equation of lien OP is $\frac{x-0}{\frac{x_1}{r}}=\frac{y-0}{\frac{y_1}{r}}=\frac{z-0}{z_1}$ Let $\mathrm{Q}(\alpha, \beta, r)$ be a point on OP such that $\mathrm{OQ}=\lambda$. Then coordinates of $Q\left(\frac{x_1 \lambda}{r}, \frac{y_1 \lambda}{r}, \frac{z_1 \lambda}{r}\right)$.
But coordinate of Q be $(\alpha, \beta, \mathrm{r})$
$\begin{aligned}
& x_1=\frac{\alpha r}{\lambda}, y_1=\frac{\beta r}{\lambda}, z_1=\frac{r^2}{\lambda} \\
& \Rightarrow a x_1+b y_1+c z_1=d \\
& \frac{r}{\lambda}(a \alpha+b \beta+c r)=d...(ii) \\
& O P \cdot O Q=d^2....(iii) \\
& (a \alpha+b \beta+c \gamma)=r=d \lambda \\
& (a \alpha+b \beta+c \gamma)=r \lambda=d \lambda^2 \\
& (a \alpha+b \beta+c \gamma)=d^2=d \lambda^2 \\
& (a \alpha+b \beta+c \gamma)=d=\lambda^2=\alpha^2+\beta^2+\gamma^2 \\
& l o c u s \text { of }(\alpha, \beta, \gamma) \text { is }(a x+b y+c z) d=x^2+y^2+z^2 \\
& \frac{(a x+b y+c z) d}{x^2+y^2+z^2}=1
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let a line parallel to $z$-axis passing through a point $P(3,4, a)$ intersects the plane $x-2 y+2 z=a^2+4 a+1$ at $Q$ where $a \in R$.
If least area of $\triangle O P Q$ is equal to $\left(\frac{p}{q}\right)$ where $p$ and $q$ are co-prime numbers, then find the value of $(p+q)$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & Q=(3,4, \lambda+a) \\ & 3-8+2(\lambda+a)=a^2+4 a+1 \\ & 2 \lambda=a^2+2 a+6 \Rightarrow \lambda=\frac{1}{2}\left(a^2+2 a+6\right) \\ & \operatorname{Ar}(\triangle O P Q)=\frac{1}{2} \times 5 \times \frac{1}{2}\left(a^2+2 a+6\right)=\frac{5}{4}\left[(a+1)^2+5\right] \\ & \therefore \text { Least area }=\frac{25}{4}=\frac{p}{q} \\ & \Rightarrow p+q=29\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: R and r are the circum-radius and in-radius of a regular tetrahedron respectively in terms of the length k of each edge. If $R^2+r^2=\frac{p}{q} k^2$, where $p, q \in I$ then absolute minimum value of $p+q$ is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text {  } \\ & \text { circum-radius } \equiv \text { distance of circum centre from any of the vertex } \\ & \equiv \text { distance of } \frac{\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}}{4} \text { from vertex } \mathrm{D}(\overrightarrow{0}) \quad \text { [tetrahedron is regular] } \\ & \text { Circumradius }=\frac{1}{4}|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}|=\frac{1}{4} \sqrt{\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}^2+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}^2+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}^2+2(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}})} \\ & =\frac{1}{4} \sqrt{\mathrm{k}^2+\mathrm{k}^2+\mathrm{k}^2+2\left(\frac{\mathrm{k}^2}{2}+\frac{\mathrm{k}^2}{2}+\frac{\mathrm{k}^2}{2}\right)}=\frac{1}{4} \sqrt{6 \mathrm{k}^2}=\sqrt{\frac{3}{8}} \mathrm{k} \\ & \frac{\mathrm{r}}{\mathrm{R}}=\frac{1}{3} \Rightarrow \mathrm{r}=\frac{\mathrm{R}}{3}=\frac{\mathrm{k}}{\sqrt{24}} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{R}=\sqrt{\frac{3}{8}} \mathrm{k} \& \mathrm{r}=\frac{\mathrm{k}}{\sqrt{24}}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow R^2+r^2=\frac{5}{12} k^2 \\ & \Rightarrow \text { minimum value of } p+q=17\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Consider a square $A B C D$ of diagonal length $2 \sqrt{3}$. The square is folded along the diagonal $A C$ so that the plane of $\triangle A B C$ is perpendicular to the plane of $\triangle A D C$. The shortest distance between $A B$ and $C D$ is $\qquad$
questions.solution: Take B as the origin, BC and BA as $x$ and $y$ axis and $z$ axis perpendicular to the plane of $A B C$ $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \overrightarrow{\mathrm{BA}}=\sqrt{6} \hat{\mathrm{j}}, \overrightarrow{\mathrm{CD}}=\left(\sqrt{\frac{3}{2}}-\sqrt{6}\right) \hat{i}+\sqrt{\frac{3}{2}} \hat{j}+\sqrt{3} \hat{k} \\ & \Rightarrow \overrightarrow{\mathrm{n}}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BA}} \times \overrightarrow{C D}=3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}+3 \sqrt{2} \hat{i}=3(\sqrt{2} \hat{i}+\hat{k})\end{aligned}$ The minimum distance $d=\left|\overrightarrow{B C} \cdot \frac{\vec{n}}{\mid \vec{n}}\right|=\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}} \cdot \sqrt{3}=2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $L_1: \frac{x-6}{3}=\frac{y-7}{-1}=\frac{z-4}{1}$ and $L_2: \frac{x}{-3}=\frac{y+9}{2}=\frac{z-2}{4}$. If $L_1 \& L_2$ are skew lines such that $P \& Q$ are two points nearest to each other lying on lines $L_1 \& L_2$ respectively. If image of $P$ with respect to plane $3 x+3 y-z=11$ is $R(a, b, c)$, then $(a+b+c)$ equals
questions.solution: direction ratio of $\begin{gathered} \mathrm{PQ} \equiv\left(-3 \mathrm{r}_2-3 \mathrm{r}_1-6,2 \mathrm{r}_2+\mathrm{r}_1-16,4 \mathrm{r}_2-\mathrm{r}_1-2\right) \\ \text { Now, }-3\left(-3 \mathrm{r}_2-3 \mathrm{r}_1-6\right)+2\left(2 \mathrm{r}_2, \mathrm{r}_1-16\right) \\ +4\left(4 \mathrm{r}_2-\mathrm{r}_1-2\right)=0 \end{gathered}$ $\begin{aligned} & 7 r_1+29 r_2=22 \\ & 3\left(-3 r_2-3 r_1-6\right)-\left(2 r_2+r_1-16\right)+\left(4 r_2-r_1-2\right)=0 \\ & \quad 7 r_1+29 r_2=22 \end{aligned}...(1)$ $\begin{gathered} 3\left(-3 r_2-3 r_1-6\right)-\left(2 r_2+r_1-16\right)+\left(4 r_2-r_1-2\right)=0 \\ 11 r_1+7 r_2=-4 \end{gathered}...(2)$ On solving (1) \& (2) $\begin{aligned} & r_1=-1, \quad r_2=1 \\ & \text { so } \quad P(3,8,3)\end{aligned}$ Image of $\mathrm{P}(3,8,3)$ w.r.t Plane $3 x+3 y-z=11$ $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{R}(-3,2,5) \\ & \mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b}+\mathrm{c}=4\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Given $f(\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}, \mathrm{c}) \leq \frac{\pi}{2}$, where $f(\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}, \mathrm{c})=\left|\frac{\mathrm{a}}{\mathrm{b}}-\frac{1}{2}\right|+(3 \mathrm{~b}-2 \mathrm{c})^2+\sin ^{-1}\left(1+\left((\mathrm{a}-1)^2+(\mathrm{b}-2)^2\right)\right)$. Also $\Pi$ denotes the plane through the line $\frac{y}{b}+\frac{z}{c}=1, x=0$ and parallel to the line $\frac{x}{a}-\frac{z}{c}=1, y=0$. If the plane $\Pi$ cuts the co-ordinate axes at $\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{B}, \mathrm{C}$, then volume of tetrahedron $\mathrm{OABC}, \mathrm{O}$ being the origin, is
questions.solution:
Clearly, $\mathrm{a}=1, \mathrm{~b}=2, \mathrm{c}=3$.
The second line can be written as
$\frac{x-a}{a}=\frac{y}{0}=\frac{z}{c}...(1)$
Now equation of plane through the line
$\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{~b}}+\frac{\mathrm{z}}{\mathrm{c}}=1, \quad \mathrm{x}=0 \text { is }\left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{~b}}+\frac{\mathrm{z}}{\mathrm{c}}-1\right)+\lambda \mathrm{x}=0$
or $\quad \lambda \mathrm{x}+\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{b}}+\frac{\mathrm{z}}{\mathrm{c}}=1...(2)$
$\because \quad$ plane $(2)$ is parallel to the line $(1)$
$\Rightarrow \lambda \cdot \mathrm{a}+\frac{1}{6} \cdot 0+\frac{1}{\mathrm{c}} \cdot \mathrm{c}=0 \Rightarrow \lambda=-\frac{1}{\mathrm{a}}$
$\therefore \quad$ equation of plane $\Pi$ is
$-\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{a}}+\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{b}}+\frac{\mathrm{z}}{\mathrm{c}}=1 \quad$ or $\quad-\frac{\mathrm{x}}{1}+\frac{\mathrm{y}}{2}+\frac{\mathrm{z}}{3}=1$
$\begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad \mathrm{A}(-1,0,0), \mathrm{B}(0,2,0), \mathrm{C}(0,0,3) \\ & \therefore \quad \text { volume of tetrahedron }\end{aligned}$
$\mathrm{OABC}=\left|\frac{1}{6}\left[\begin{array}{lll}-\hat{\mathrm{i}} & 2 \hat{\mathrm{j}} & 3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}\end{array}\right]\right|=6 \cdot \frac{1}{6}[\hat{\mathrm{i}} \hat{\mathrm{j}} \hat{\mathrm{k}}]=1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the coordinates of the point where the line $x-2 y+z-1=0=x+2 y-2 z-5$ intersects the plane $x+y-2 z=7$ is $(\alpha, \beta, \gamma)$, then find the value of $(|\alpha|+|\beta|+|\gamma|)$.
questions.solution:
The required point is the point of intersection of the three planes.
$\begin{aligned}
& x+y-2 z=7 \quad ...(1)\\
& x-2 y+z=1 \quad ...(2)\\
& x+2 y-2 z=5 \quad ...(3)
\end{aligned}$
$\therefore$ From (3)-(1) $\qquad \Rightarrow \mathrm{y}=-2$
From $2 \times(2)+(1)$ $\qquad \Rightarrow 3 \mathrm{x}-3 \mathrm{y}=9$
$\Rightarrow x=1$
So, from (1),$z=-4$
Hence the point is $(1,-2,-4)=(\alpha, \beta, \gamma)$
Hence $|\alpha|+|\beta|+|\gamma|=|1|+|-2|+|-4|=7$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
